text: Na Jakob hun h…în no …îsee d…õ, Nyankop…în no d…îm nye yi: na …îfr…õ…õ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Na Jakob tuu ab…îfo ma wodzii n‚Äôenyim kan k…îr no nua Esau h…î w…î Seir asaase do, Edom haban mu.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, ‚ÄúMaster, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Na …îhy…õ…õ h…în d…õ, Hom nk…õse mo wura Esau d…õm; d…õ, Mbr…õ w‚Äôakowaa Jakob se nye yi, Menye Laban ak…õtsena, na maky…õr h…î ara ma ebodu nd…õ.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.‚Äù
text: Menya anantwi, nsoasop…înk…î, nguan, mp…înkye na nkowaa nye mfonabaa: na masoma d…õ w…îmb…õka nkyer…õ mo wura, ama meenya w‚Äôenyim adom.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, ‚ÄúWe went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Na ab…îfo no sanee baa Jakob h…î, d…õ, Y…õk…îr wo nua Esau h…î na mbom …înoara so reba ebehyia wo kwan, na mbarimba ahaanan ka no ho.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Nna Jakob n‚Äôakoma tuu k…õsenara, na no ho hiahiaa no. Na obuee nkor…îfo a w…înye no nam no, nye mp…înkye, nguan, anantwi nye efump…înk…î no h…în mu ekuw ebien;

ref: He thought, ‚ÄúIf Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.‚Äù
text: na …îsee d…õ, S…õ Esau ba kuw kor do, na oboku h…în a, …îno ikuw a …îaka no eefi nsa.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Na Jakob see d…õ, Ao, m‚Äôegya Abraham ne Nyankop…în, na m‚Äôegya Isaak ne Nyankop…în, ao, EWURADZE a esee me d…õ, San k…î wo man mu, nye w‚Äôebusua mu, na meb…õy…õ wo yie:

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Meser…õ wo, gye me fi mo nua Esau nsamu: na musuro no, b…õy…õ na …îaba eboku emi, nye enanom nye h…în mba yi.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Na esee me d…õ, Ampaara meb…õy…õ wo yie, na mama w‚Äôasefo ay…õ d…õ mpoano anhwea a no dodow ntsi w…îkan a …înny…õ.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Na …îso…õ…õ h…î anafua no; na oyii dza …îw…î ne nky…õn no mu bi d…õ …îdze rek…õky…õ no nua Esau;

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Na hom nse d…õ, Nkyii so, hw…õ w‚Äôakowaa Jakob so dzi h…õn ekyir. Afei …îkaa …îsee d…õ, Medze aky…õdze a odzi m‚Äôenyim yi b…îk…î ak…îdwedwe no, na n‚Äôekyir no meehu n‚Äôenyim; bi a no bo b…îdwe.

ref: Jacob said, ‚ÄúI have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.‚Äù So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Jakob fr…õ…õ bea no ne dzin Peniel; osiand…õ, …îsee d…õ, Mehu Nyankop…în mpu na mpu, naaso me kra enya nkwa.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, ‚ÄúGo ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.‚Äù
text: Na …îdze no ekuwekuw hy…õ…õ no nkowaa no nsa; na …îsee no nkowaa no d…õ, Hom ntwa mu ndzi m‚Äôenyim, na hom mma ikuw nye ikuw ntamu mbra kwan.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, ‚ÄúWhen Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Na …îhy…õ…õ dza odzi kan no d…õ, S…õ mo nua Esau nye wo hyia, na obisa wo d…õ, Woana n‚Äôabofra nye wo, erok…î henfa, na dza wodzi w‚Äôenyim yi woana dze a?

ref: So tell him, ‚ÄòThey belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.‚Äô ‚Äù
text: ∆Üno se d…õ, W‚Äôakowaa Jakob dze a; …îy…õ aky…õdze a w…îdze rek…õma mo wura Esau: na, hw…õ, …înoara so dzi h…õn ekyir.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Na …îhy…õ…õ dza otsia ebien no so, nye dza otsia ebiasa no, nye h…în a w…îka mbowakuw no do no nyinara d…õ, S…õ hom hu Esau a, w…înka d…õm as…õm yi ara nkyer…õ no.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Nna aky…õdze no twaa mu dzii n‚Äôenyim: na …înoara dze, …îtsenaa d…îm no mu anafua no. Jakob N‚Äôaperdzi W…î Peniel

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Na …îkaa Jakob no nko; na banyin bi nye no dzii aper w…î h…î ara ma okodur adzekyee.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na nyimpa no hun d…õ onndzi Jakob do konyim no, nna …îkaa n‚Äôaser…õ tokur mu; na Jakob no ndwonku huanee w…î aperper a …înye no dzii no mu.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, ‚ÄúLet go of me! It's almost daylight.‚Äù ‚ÄúYou can't go until you bless me,‚Äù Jacob replied.
text: Na banyin no see d…õ, Ma monk…î, na adze rekye. Na …îsee d…õ, Mennk…õma ak…î, gyed…õ ehyira me ana.

ref: Then the man asked, ‚ÄúWhat is your name?‚Äù ‚ÄúJacob,‚Äù he answered.
text: Na banyin no bisaa no d…õ, Wo dzin dze d…õn? Na obuaa d…õ, Jakob.

ref: The man said, ‚ÄúFrom now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.‚Äù
text: Na …îsee d…õ, W…înnk…õfr…õ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiand…õ …îwo enye Nyankop…în na …înye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.

ref: Jacob said, ‚ÄúNow tell me your name.‚Äù ‚ÄúDon't you know who I am?‚Äù he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Na Jakob bisaa no d…õ, Meser…õ wo, b…î wo dzin kyer…õ me. Na …îsee d…õ, Na iribisa me dzin ay…õ d…õn? Na banyin no hyiraa no w…î h…î.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Na orutwa mu w…î Peniel no nna ewia epue, na no ndwonku a ehuan no ntsi nna ohuan n‚Äôaser…õ mu.

text: Na Moses tseen no poma no w…î Egypt asaase do, na EWURADZE dze boka mframa b…î…î asaase no do da no nyinara, na anadwe no nyinara; na anapa no, boka mframa dze mb…õb…õw no bae.

text: Na w…îkataa asaase no nyinara enyi, ma isum gyee asaase no do; na w…îwee asaase no do efuw nyinara, nye ndua a …îsorbo no gyaae no do aba nyinara: na adze momon biara annka a …îy…õ dua anaa haban mu efuw, w…î Egypt asaase nyinara do.

ref: At once the king sent for Moses and Aaron. He told them, ‚ÄúI have sinned against the Lord your God and against you.

text: D…õm ntsi afei, mepa wo ky…õw, fa mo b…în fir me pr…õko yi ara, na hom mpa EWURADZE hom Nyankop…în ky…õw, ma onyi owu yi ara mfi mo do.

text: Na ofii Farao nky…õn k…îree, na …îk…õpaa EWURADZE ky…õw.

text: Na EWURADZE piriim Farao n‚Äôakoma, na …îamma Israelmba no annk…î. Sum

text: Na Moses see d…õ, Aka no yie; munnkohu w‚Äôenyim bio.

text: Na EWURADZE see Moses d…õ, Meb…õma …îyard…îm kor so aba Farao, nye Egypt do bio; na n‚Äôekyir no …îb…õma hom efi ha ak…î: aber a …îb…õma hom ak…î no, ampaara, …îb…õpam hom efi ha koraa.

ref: So the Lord made the Egyptians greatly respect the Israelites, and everyone, including the king's officials, considered Moses an important leader.
text: Na EWURADZE maa …îman no enyimpa w…î Egyptfo enyim. Nkyii so nna …îy…õ…õ Moses otsitsir paara w…î Egypt asaase do, w…î Farao nkowaa enyim, nye …îman no enyim.

ref: Moses went to the king and said: I have come to let you know what the Lord is going to do. About midnight he will go through the land of Egypt,
text: Na Moses see d…õ, Mbr…õ EWURADZE se nye yi, B…õy…õ esuom na mibefir edzi mafa Egypt mu:

ref: and wherever he goes, the first-born son in every family will die. Your own son will die, and so will the son of the lowest slave woman. Even the first-born males of your cattle will die.
text: na Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara bowuwu, ofi Farao n‚Äôabakan a …îtsena n‚Äôahengua do do, dze kodu afonabaa mpo a …îw…î ewuyambo ekyir n‚Äôabakan do; nye ofiembowa mbakan nyinara.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt there will be loud crying. Nothing like this has ever happened before or will ever happen again.
text: Na su k…õse b…õba Egypt asaase nyinara do a ne saso bi mbaa da, eso ne saso bi nnk…õba bio.

ref: But there won't be any need for the Israelites to cry. Things will be so quiet that not even a dog will be heard barking. Then you Egyptians will know that the Lord is good to the Israelites, even while he punishes you.
text: Na Israelmba nyinara dze, …îkraman mpo nnk…îtwe ne t…õkyer…õma ahye h…în, …înnk…õhye nyimpa anaa abowa: ama hom eehu mbr…õ EWURADZE si dze …îhe to Egyptfo nye Israel ntamu.

ref: Your leaders will come and bow down, begging me to take my people and leave your country. Then we will leave. Moses was very angry; he turned and left the king.
text: Na wo nkowaa yi nyinara besian aba mo h…î, na w…îab…îb…î h…în mu adze ama me d…õ, Fir edzi k…î, …îwo nye …îman a w…îka wo do nyinara: na …îno ekyir no na mibefir edzi mak…î. Na …îdze ebufuhyew fii Farao nky…õn k…îree.

ref: What the Lord had earlier said to Moses came true. He had said, ‚ÄúThe king of Egypt won't listen. Then I will perform even more miracles.‚Äù
text: Na EWURADZE see Moses d…õ, Farao nnketsie hom; ama m‚Äôanwanwadze aad…î…î so w…î Egypt asaase do.

ref: So the king of Egypt saw Moses and Aaron work miracles, but the Lord made him stubbornly refuse to let the Israelites leave his country.
text: Na Moses nye Aaron y…õ…õ anwanwadze yi nyinara w…î Farao enyim; na EWURADZE piriim Farao n‚Äôakoma, na …îamma Israelmba emmfi n‚Äôasaase do annk…î.

ref: Some time later the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
text: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyer…õ…õ Moses nye Aaron w…î Egypt asaase do d…õ,

ref: This month is to be the first month of the year for you.
text: Bosoom yi b…õy…õ abosoom ahy…õse ama hom: …îb…õy…õ afrenhyia mu bosoom a odzi kan ama hom.

text: Hom nkasa nkyer…õ Israel mboanofo no nyinara d…õ, Bosoom yi ne da a otsia du no mu, w…îmfefa nguambaa nkorkor, d…õ mbr…õ hom egyanom efiefi tsetsee, fie kor eguambaa kor:

ref: Each family must take care of its animal until the evening of the fourteenth day of the month, when the animals are to be killed.
text: na hom mfa no nsie ma onkodu bosoom kor no ara ne da a otsia duanan no: na Israel mboanofo kuw no nyina nkuku h…în ewimbir.

text: Mma wonndzi no amon, nkyii so mma w…îmmfa nsu nnoa, mbom w…îmfa gya ntoto; ne tsir nye no ntw…õr na …înye n‚Äôayamudze no.

ref: Eat what you want that night, and the next morning burn whatever is left.
text: Na mma hom mma no ho biribiara nnka ma adze nnkye do; na dza …îb…õka ma adze akye do no hom mfa gya nhyew.

ref: When you eat the meal, be dressed and ready to travel. Have your sandals on, carry your walking stick in your hand, and eat quickly. This is the Passover Festival in honor of me, your Lord.
text: Na mbr…õ hom ndzi no nye yi; hom mbob…î hom asen do, hom mpaboa nhyehy…õ hom anan, na hom mpoma nkitsakitsa hom nsamu: na hom ndzi no …îhar do: …îy…õ EWURADZE apaho.

ref: That same night I will pass through Egypt and kill the first-born son in every family and the first-born male of all animals. I am the Lord, and I will punish the gods of Egypt.
text: Osiand…õ mubotwa mu w…î Egypt asaase do anadwe no, na muboku Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara, nyimpa nye mbowa nyina; na mubobu Egypt enyame nyinara nts…õn: emi nye EWURADZE.

ref: The blood on the houses will show me where you live, and when I see the blood, I will pass over you. Then you won't be bothered by the terrible disasters I will bring on Egypt.
text: Na b…îgya no b…õy…õ as…õnkyer…õdze ama hom w…î efi a hom w…î mu no ho: na s…õ muhu mb…îgya no a, meb…õpa hom ho, na …îyard…îm bi nnk…õba hom do ab…õs…õ…õ hom, s…õ morob…îb…î Egypt asaase no a.

ref: Remember this day and celebrate it each year as a festival in my honor.
text: Na da yi ny…õ nkaa mma hom, na hom nhy…õ no fa mma EWURADZE: hom mfa mbra do ny…õ no afahy…õ w…î hom ebusuasantsen mu daa.

ref: For seven days you must eat bread made without yeast. And on the first of these seven days, you must remove all yeast from your homes. If you eat anything made with yeast during this festival, you will no longer be part of Israel.
text: Nda esuon na hom ndzi epitsi boodoo; da a odzi kan no ara na hom nyi mb…îrkaw mfi hom efiefi: na obiara a obedzi mb…îrkaw boodoo ofitsi da a odzi kan no do dze kodu da a otsia esuon no do no, …îkra no wobotwa no efi Israel.

ref: Meet together for worship on the first and seventh days of the festival. The only work you are allowed to do on either of these two days is that of preparing the bread.
text: Na da a odzi kan no hom ny…õ no nhyiamu kr…înkr…în, na da a otsia esuon no so nhyiamu kr…înkr…în; mma w…înny…õ edwuma biara w…î mu, gyed…õ dza nyimpa biara bedzi nko na hom ny…õ.

ref: Celebrate this Festival of Thin Bread as a way of remembering the day that I brought your families and tribes out of Egypt. And do this each year.
text: Na hom ndwen epitsi afahy…õ no do; na d…õm da yi ara so na miyii hom d…îm no mifii Egypt asaase do: d…õm ntsi hom ndwen da yi do w…î hom ebusuasantsen mu ny…õ no mbra daa.

ref: Begin on the evening of the fourteenth day of the first month by eating bread made without yeast. Then continue this celebration until the evening of the twenty-first day.
text: Bosoom a odzi kan no mu, bosoom no mu da a otsia duanan ewimbir no, na hom ndzi epitsi ma onkodu bosoom no mu da a otsia eduonu biako ewimbir.

ref: During these seven days no yeast is allowed in anyone's home, whether they are native Israelites or not. If you are caught eating anything made with yeast, you will no longer be part of Israel.
text: Nda esuon na mma mma wonnhu mb…îrkaw w…î hom efi mu: na obiara a obedzi dza …îaka no, …îkra no wobotwa no efi Israel mboanofo mu, s…õ …îy…õ …îmamforanyi o, obi a w…îwoo no w…î asaase no do o.

ref: Stay away from yeast, no matter where you live. No one is allowed to eat anything made with yeast!
text: Mma hom nndzi biribiara a akaw; hom ndzi epitsi w…î hom tsenabew nyinara.

ref: Moses called the leaders of Israel together and said: Each family is to pick out a sheep and kill it for Passover.
text: Nna Moses fr…õ…õ Israel mpanyimfo nyinara, na …îsee h…în d…õ, Hom nk…î nkeyiyi nguambaa d…õ mbr…õ hom ebusua tsetsee, na hom nku apaho no.

ref: Make a brush from a few small branches of a hyssop plant and dip the brush in the bowl that has the blood of the animal in it. Then brush some of the blood above the door and on the posts at each side of the door of your house. After this, everyone is to stay inside until morning.
text: Na hom mfa odwonhataw siaw, na hom mfa mb…î b…îgya a …îw…î b…õ…õsen no mu no mu, na hom mfa b…îgya a …îw…î b…õ…õsen no mu no bi mpetse mpondua etsifi nye apondua ebien no ho; na mma hom mu obiara mmfir edzi mmfi ne fie abowano ma onkodu anapa.

ref: During that night the Lord will go through the country of Egypt and kill the first-born son in every Egyptian family. He will see where you have put the blood, and he will not come into your house. His angel that brings death will pass over and not kill your first-born sons.
text: Na EWURADZE botwa mu ab…îb…î Egyptfo; na s…õ ohu b…îgya no w…î mpondua etsifi, nye apondua ebien no ho a, EWURADZE b…õpa …îpon no ho, na …înnk…õma …îs…õ…õfo no kwan ma …îaaba hom efi mu ab…îbob…î hom.

ref: After you have entered the country promised to you by the Lord, you and your children must continue to celebrate Passover each year.
text: Na …îb…õba d…õ, s…õ hom du asaase a EWURADZE dze b…õma hom no do, d…õ mbr…õ …îab…î anohoba no a, …îno hom ndzi …îsom yi do.

ref: Your children will ask you, ‚ÄúWhat are we celebrating?‚Äù
text: Na …îb…õba d…õ, s…õ hom mba bisa hom d…õ, Hom som yi ase nye d…õn?

ref: And you will answer, ‚ÄúThe Passover animal is killed to honor the Lord. We do these things because on that night long ago the Lord passed over the homes of our people in Egypt. He killed the first-born sons of the Egyptians, but he spared our children from death.‚Äù After Moses finished speaking, the people of Israel knelt down and worshiped the Lord.
text: ∆Üno hom nse d…õ, ∆Üy…õ EWURADZE n‚Äôapaho af…îrb…îdze, nyia …îpaa Israelmba h…în efi ho w…î Egypt, w…î aber a …îb…î…î Egyptfo, na oyii h…õn efi fii mu no. Na …îman no b…î…î h…în tsir adze na w…îs…îree.

ref: Then they left and did what Moses and Aaron had told them to do. Death for the First-Born Sons
text: Na Israelmba k…îree k…õy…õ…õ d…õ mbr…õ EWURADZE hy…õ…õ Moses nye Aaron no. Mbakamba H…în Wu

ref: At midnight the Lord killed the first-born son of every Egyptian family, from the son of the king to the son of every prisoner in jail. He also killed the first-born male of every animal that belonged to the Egyptians.
text: Na …îbaa d…õ esuom no, EWURADZE b…îb…î…î Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara, ofi Farao n‚Äôabakan a …îtsena n‚Äôahengua do no do dze kodu adaduadan mu nd…îmmum n‚Äôabakan do; nye ofiembowa mbakan nyinara.

ref: That night the king, his officials, and everyone else in Egypt got up and started crying bitterly. In every Egyptian home, someone was dead. The People of Israel Escape from Egypt
text: Na Farao so…õ…õr anadwe no ara, …îno, nye no nkowaa nyinara, nye Egyptfo nyinara: na su k…õse baa Egypt; osiand…õ fie biara nnyi h…î a obi ennwu w…î mu.

ref: During the night the king sent for Moses and Aaron and told them, ‚ÄúGet your people out of my country and leave us alone! Go and worship the Lord, as you have asked.
text: Na …îfr…õ…õ Moses nye Aaron anadwe, na …îsee d…õ, Hom nso…õr, hom mfi me man mu mfir, hom nye Israelmba no nyina; na hom nk…î nk…îsom EWURADZE, d…õ mbr…õ hom aka no.

ref: Take your sheep, goats, and cattle, and get out. But ask your God to be kind to me.‚Äù
text: Hom mfa hom nguan nye hom anantwi so, d…õ mbr…õ hom aka no, na hom nk…î; na hom nhyira mo so.

ref: The Egyptians did everything they could to get the Israelites to leave their country as quickly as possible. They said, ‚ÄúPlease hurry and leave. If you don't, we will all be dead.‚Äù
text: Na Egyptfo no piapiaa …îman no d…õ w…îrepam h…în nts…õm efi asaase no do; na w…îsee d…õ, H…õn nyinara ay…õ ewufo ara nye no.

ref: So the Israelites quickly made some bread dough and put it in pans. But they did not mix any yeast in the dough to make it rise. They wrapped cloth around the pans and carried them on their shoulders.
text: Na …îman no fefaa h…în mb…îr ansaana …îrekaw, h…în mb…îrakorow no w…îakyekyer no w…î h…în atam mu sisi h…în k…în ho.

ref: The Israelites had already done what Moses had told them to do. They had gone to their Egyptian neighbors and asked for gold and silver and for clothes.
text: Na Israelmba no y…õ…õ d…õ mbr…õ Moses kae no; na wobisabisaa dwet…õ ndwindze, nye sika ndwindze, nye atam w…î Egyptfo h…î:

ref: The Lord had made the Egyptians friendly toward the people of Israel, and they gave them whatever they asked for. In this way they carried away the wealth of the Egyptians when they left Egypt.
text: na EWURADZE maa …îman no enyimpa w…î Egyptfo no enyim, ma w…îmaa h…în nsa kaa dza wobisae no. Na w…îfoom Egyptfo egyapadze k…îree. Israelmba No Fi Egypt

ref: The Israelites walked from the city of Rameses to the city of Succoth. There were about
text: Na Israelmba no sii mu fii Rameses k…îr Suk…ît, mbanyin ara a w…înam famu b…õy…õ mpem ahaesia, a mbofraba nnka ho.

ref: Many other people went with them as well, and there were also a lot of sheep, goats, and cattle.
text: Na aforafora d…îm so nye h…în k…îree; na nguan, na anantwi, mekyer…õ ofiembowa piigyan.

ref: The Lord promised your ancestors that he would bring you into the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Hivites, and Jebusites. It is a land rich with milk and honey. Each year during the month of Abib, celebrate these events in the following way:

ref: From then on, you must give him every first-born son from your families and every first-born male from your animals, because these belong to him.

ref: In the future your children will ask what this ceremony means. Explain it to them by saying, ‚ÄúThe Lord used his mighty power to rescue us from slavery in Egypt.

text: Na …îbaa d…õ, aber a nna Farao mmpen d…õ …îb…õma y…õak…î no, EWURADZE kunkum Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara, nyimpa abakan, nye mbowa abakan nyina: d…õm ntsi na mo so medze enyin nyinara a wobue yafun mu no mob…î EWURADZE af…îr; na me mbabanyin mu mbakan nyinara dze medze mbowa sesa gye h…în.

ref: This ceremony will serve the same purpose as a sign on your hand or on your forehead to tell how the Lord's mighty power rescued us from Egypt. The Lord Leads His People
text: Na …îb…õy…õ wo nsa do as…õnkyer…õdze, nye w‚Äôenyiwa ntamu nkaadze: na nsa a …îy…õ dzen na EWURADZE dze yii h…õn fii Egypt. Ogya Na Mununkum Fadum

text: Mma w‚Äôenyi mmber wo ny…õnko fi, mma w‚Äôenyi mmber ne yer, anaa n‚Äôakowaa, anaa n‚Äôafonabaa, anaa ne nantwi, anaa n‚Äôasoaso, anaa biribiara a …îy…õ wo ny…õnko dze. Suro Ka ∆Üman No (Deuteronomi

text: Na iyinom nye nts…õmbus…õm a fa to h…în enyim.

ref: If he fails to do any of these things, she must be given her freedom without paying for it. Murder and Other Violent Crimes The Lord said:
text: Na s…õ …îanny…õ iyinom ebiasa yi amma no a, …înodze …îmfa noho ndzi nk…î kwa, sika bi nnyi mu. Basabasay…õ a ∆Üdze Pira Anaa Owu Ba

text: Na nyia owia nyimpa, na …ît…în no no, anaa s…õ wohu no w…î ne nsamu a, oku na wonku no.

text: Na nyia …îdom n‚Äôegya, anaa ne na no, oku na wonku no.

ref: and later has to use a stick when walking outside, you must pay for the loss of time and do what you can to help until the injury is completely healed. That's your only responsibility.
text: s…õ …îso…õr bio, na osuo no poma mu nantsew fir edzi a, …înodze nyia …îb…î…î no no no ho ny…õ no t…îtr…ît…î: ne mber a as…õ…õ no nko na ontua kaw, na …îmma w…însa no yarba koraa.

text: Na s…õ nyimpa bob…î n‚Äôakowaa, anaa n‚Äôafonabaa abaa, ma owu ne nsaano a; …îno wondzi no wer.

ref: The same law applies if you knock out a slave's tooth‚Äîthe slave goes free.
text: Na s…õ …îb…î n‚Äôakowaa ne se tu no, anaa n‚Äôafonabaa ne se a; ne se no ntsi …îmma …îmfa noho ndzi nk…î. Ewuranom H…în Asodzii

text: Na s…õ …înantwi s…õ …îbanyin anaa …îbaa, ma owu a, …îno …înantwi no wonsiw no abo, na mma wonndzi ne nam; na …înantwi no wura dze wongyaa no.

text: S…õ …înantwi no s…õ akowaa anaa afonabaa a; …îno …îmma h…în wura dwet…õ dwet…õbona eduasa, na wonsiw …înantwi no abo.

ref: If you refuse to fence in a bull that is known to attack others, you must replace any animal it kills, but the dead animal will belong to you.

ref: If you steal an ox and slaughter or sell it, you must replace it with five oxen; if you steal a sheep and slaughter it or sell it, you must replace it with four sheep.

ref: If you allow any of your animals to stray from your property and graze in someone else's field or vineyard, you must repay the damage from the best part of your own harvest of grapes and grain.

text: Na ofii Farao nky…õn k…îree, na …îk…õpaa EWURADZE ky…õw.

ref: Then the Lord sent a strong west wind that swept the locusts into the Red Sea. Not one locust was left anywhere in Egypt,
text: Na EWURADZE dze daan anee mframa dzendzen mbordo a …îb…õfaa mb…õb…õw no, na …îdze h…în koguu Mb…õw Po no mu; ab…õb…õw kor mpo annka w…î Egypt ntoaado nyinara.

ref: but the Lord made the king so stubborn that he still refused to let the Israelites go. Darkness
text: Na EWURADZE piriim Farao n‚Äôakoma, na …îamma Israelmba no annk…î. Sum

ref: The Lord said to Moses, ‚ÄúStretch your arm toward the sky, and everything will be covered with darkness thick enough to touch.‚Äù
text: Na EWURADZE see Moses d…õ, Tsen wo nsa w…î sor, ma sum mbra Egypt asaase do, mekyer…õ sum a wosuo mu a …îb…õy…õ a yie.

text: Na Moses tseen ne nsa w…î sor; na sum kebii hy…õ…õ Egypt asaase nyinara dze, do nda ebiasa;

ref: During that time, the Egyptians could not see each other or leave their homes, but there was light where the Israelites lived.
text: obiara ennhu ne ny…õnko, nkyii so obiara annso…õr emmfi ne tsenabew nda ebiasa: na Israelmba nyinara wonyaa kan w…î h…în efiefi.

text: Na Farao fr…õ…õ Moses, na …îsee d…õ, Hom nk…î nk…îsom EWURADZE; hom nguan nye hom anantwi nko na w…îmma w…înka: w…îmma hom mbofraba no so nye hom nk…î.

ref: ‚ÄúNo!‚Äù Moses replied. ‚ÄúYou must let us offer sacrifices to the Lord our God,
text: Na Moses see d…õ, Ohia d…õ edze okum af…îrb…îdze nye …îhyew af…îrb…îdze so hy…õ h…õn nsa ama yaab…î af…îr ama EWURADZE h…õn Nyankop…în.

ref: and we won't know which animals we will need until we get there. That's why we can't leave even one of them here.‚Äù
text: H…õn mbowa so nye h…õn b…îk…î; …înnk…õka abowaanam…în mpo w…î ekyir; na …îno mu bi na otwar d…õ y…õk…õfa som EWURADZE h…õn Nyankop…în; na yennyim dza y…õdze b…îsom EWURADZE, gyed…õ yedu h…î ana.

ref: This time the Lord made the king so stubborn
text: Na EWURADZE piriim Farao n‚Äôakoma, na …îammp…õ d…õ …îma w…îk…î.

ref: that he said to Moses, ‚ÄúGet out and stay out! If you ever come back, you're dead!‚Äù
text: Na Farao see no d…õ, Fi mo do k…î, hw…õ woho yie, mma nnhu m‚Äôenyim bio; na da a ibohu m‚Äôenyim no ibowu.

ref: ‚ÄúHave it your way,‚Äù Moses answered. ‚ÄúYou won't see me again.‚Äù
text: Na Moses see d…õ, Aka no yie; munnkohu w‚Äôenyim bio.

ref: The Lord said to Moses: I am going to punish the king of Egypt and his people one more time. Then the king will gladly let you leave his land. In fact, he will even chase you out.
text: Na EWURADZE see Moses d…õ, Meb…õma …îyard…îm kor so aba Farao, nye Egypt do bio; na n‚Äôekyir no …îb…õma hom efi ha ak…î: aber a …îb…õma hom ak…î no, ampaara, …îb…õpam hom efi ha koraa.

ref: Now go and tell my people to ask their Egyptian neighbors for gold and silver jewelry.
text: Afei kasa w…î …îman no asomu, na ma …îbanyin biara mbisa w…î ne ny…õnko h…î, na …îbaa mbisa w…î ne ny…õnko h…î, dwet…õ ndwindze, nye sika ndwindze.

ref: So the Lord made the Egyptians greatly respect the Israelites, and everyone, including the king's officials, considered Moses an important leader.
text: Na EWURADZE maa …îman no enyimpa w…î Egyptfo enyim. Nkyii so nna …îy…õ…õ Moses otsitsir paara w…î Egypt asaase do, w…î Farao nkowaa enyim, nye …îman no enyim.

ref: Moses went to the king and said: I have come to let you know what the Lord is going to do. About midnight he will go through the land of Egypt,
text: Na Moses see d…õ, Mbr…õ EWURADZE se nye yi, B…õy…õ esuom na mibefir edzi mafa Egypt mu:

ref: and wherever he goes, the first-born son in every family will die. Your own son will die, and so will the son of the lowest slave woman. Even the first-born males of your cattle will die.
text: na Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara bowuwu, ofi Farao n‚Äôabakan a …îtsena n‚Äôahengua do do, dze kodu afonabaa mpo a …îw…î ewuyambo ekyir n‚Äôabakan do; nye ofiembowa mbakan nyinara.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt there will be loud crying. Nothing like this has ever happened before or will ever happen again.
text: Na su k…õse b…õba Egypt asaase nyinara do a ne saso bi mbaa da, eso ne saso bi nnk…õba bio.

ref: But there won't be any need for the Israelites to cry. Things will be so quiet that not even a dog will be heard barking. Then you Egyptians will know that the Lord is good to the Israelites, even while he punishes you.
text: Na Israelmba nyinara dze, …îkraman mpo nnk…îtwe ne t…õkyer…õma ahye h…în, …înnk…õhye nyimpa anaa abowa: ama hom eehu mbr…õ EWURADZE si dze …îhe to Egyptfo nye Israel ntamu.

ref: Your leaders will come and bow down, begging me to take my people and leave your country. Then we will leave. Moses was very angry; he turned and left the king.
text: Na wo nkowaa yi nyinara besian aba mo h…î, na w…îab…îb…î h…în mu adze ama me d…õ, Fir edzi k…î, …îwo nye …îman a w…îka wo do nyinara: na …îno ekyir no na mibefir edzi mak…î. Na …îdze ebufuhyew fii Farao nky…õn k…îree.

ref: What the Lord had earlier said to Moses came true. He had said, ‚ÄúThe king of Egypt won't listen. Then I will perform even more miracles.‚Äù
text: Na EWURADZE see Moses d…õ, Farao nnketsie hom; ama m‚Äôanwanwadze aad…î…î so w…î Egypt asaase do.

ref: So the king of Egypt saw Moses and Aaron work miracles, but the Lord made him stubbornly refuse to let the Israelites leave his country.
text: Na Moses nye Aaron y…õ…õ anwanwadze yi nyinara w…î Farao enyim; na EWURADZE piriim Farao n‚Äôakoma, na …îamma Israelmba emmfi n‚Äôasaase do annk…î.

ref: Some time later the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
text: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyer…õ…õ Moses nye Aaron w…î Egypt asaase do d…õ,

ref: This month is to be the first month of the year for you.
text: Bosoom yi b…õy…õ abosoom ahy…õse ama hom: …îb…õy…õ afrenhyia mu bosoom a odzi kan ama hom.

ref: Tell the people of Israel that on the tenth day of this month the head of each family must choose a lamb or a young goat for his family to eat.
text: Hom nkasa nkyer…õ Israel mboanofo no nyinara d…õ, Bosoom yi ne da a otsia du no mu, w…îmfefa nguambaa nkorkor, d…õ mbr…õ hom egyanom efiefi tsetsee, fie kor eguambaa kor:

ref: Each family must take care of its animal until the evening of the fourteenth day of the month, when the animals are to be killed.
text: na hom mfa no nsie ma onkodu bosoom kor no ara ne da a otsia duanan no: na Israel mboanofo kuw no nyina nkuku h…în ewimbir.

ref: Some of the blood must be put on the two doorposts and above the door of each house where the animals are to be eaten.
text: Na w…îmfa b…îgya no bi, na w…îmfa nsera efi a wobedzi no w…î mu no n‚Äôapondua ebien no nye mpondua etsifi no ho.

ref: That night the animals are to be roasted and eaten, together with bitter herbs and thin bread made without yeast.
text: Na wondzi nam no anadwe no ara, w…îmfa gya ntoto, na w…îmfa epitsi boodoo, nye fan wenwen ndzi.

ref: Don't eat the meat raw or boiled. The entire animal, including its head, legs, and insides, must be roasted.
text: Mma wonndzi no amon, nkyii so mma w…îmmfa nsu nnoa, mbom w…îmfa gya ntoto; ne tsir nye no ntw…õr na …înye n‚Äôayamudze no.

ref: Eat what you want that night, and the next morning burn whatever is left.
text: Na mma hom mma no ho biribiara nnka ma adze nnkye do; na dza …îb…õka ma adze akye do no hom mfa gya nhyew.

ref: When you eat the meal, be dressed and ready to travel. Have your sandals on, carry your walking stick in your hand, and eat quickly. This is the Passover Festival in honor of me, your Lord.
text: Na mbr…õ hom ndzi no nye yi; hom mbob…î hom asen do, hom mpaboa nhyehy…õ hom anan, na hom mpoma nkitsakitsa hom nsamu: na hom ndzi no …îhar do: …îy…õ EWURADZE apaho.

ref: That same night I will pass through Egypt and kill the first-born son in every family and the first-born male of all animals. I am the Lord, and I will punish the gods of Egypt.
text: Osiand…õ mubotwa mu w…î Egypt asaase do anadwe no, na muboku Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara, nyimpa nye mbowa nyina; na mubobu Egypt enyame nyinara nts…õn: emi nye EWURADZE.

ref: The blood on the houses will show me where you live, and when I see the blood, I will pass over you. Then you won't be bothered by the terrible disasters I will bring on Egypt.
text: Na b…îgya no b…õy…õ as…õnkyer…õdze ama hom w…î efi a hom w…î mu no ho: na s…õ muhu mb…îgya no a, meb…õpa hom ho, na …îyard…îm bi nnk…õba hom do ab…õs…õ…õ hom, s…õ morob…îb…î Egypt asaase no a.

ref: Remember this day and celebrate it each year as a festival in my honor.
text: Na da yi ny…õ nkaa mma hom, na hom nhy…õ no fa mma EWURADZE: hom mfa mbra do ny…õ no afahy…õ w…î hom ebusuasantsen mu daa.

ref: For seven days you must eat bread made without yeast. And on the first of these seven days, you must remove all yeast from your homes. If you eat anything made with yeast during this festival, you will no longer be part of Israel.
text: Nda esuon na hom ndzi epitsi boodoo; da a odzi kan no ara na hom nyi mb…îrkaw mfi hom efiefi: na obiara a obedzi mb…îrkaw boodoo ofitsi da a odzi kan no do dze kodu da a otsia esuon no do no, …îkra no wobotwa no efi Israel.

ref: Meet together for worship on the first and seventh days of the festival. The only work you are allowed to do on either of these two days is that of preparing the bread.
text: Na da a odzi kan no hom ny…õ no nhyiamu kr…înkr…în, na da a otsia esuon no so nhyiamu kr…înkr…în; mma w…înny…õ edwuma biara w…î mu, gyed…õ dza nyimpa biara bedzi nko na hom ny…õ.

ref: Celebrate this Festival of Thin Bread as a way of remembering the day that I brought your families and tribes out of Egypt. And do this each year.
text: Na hom ndwen epitsi afahy…õ no do; na d…õm da yi ara so na miyii hom d…îm no mifii Egypt asaase do: d…õm ntsi hom ndwen da yi do w…î hom ebusuasantsen mu ny…õ no mbra daa.

ref: Begin on the evening of the fourteenth day of the first month by eating bread made without yeast. Then continue this celebration until the evening of the twenty-first day.
text: Bosoom a odzi kan no mu, bosoom no mu da a otsia duanan ewimbir no, na hom ndzi epitsi ma onkodu bosoom no mu da a otsia eduonu biako ewimbir.

ref: During these seven days no yeast is allowed in anyone's home, whether they are native Israelites or not. If you are caught eating anything made with yeast, you will no longer be part of Israel.
text: Nda esuon na mma mma wonnhu mb…îrkaw w…î hom efi mu: na obiara a obedzi dza …îaka no, …îkra no wobotwa no efi Israel mboanofo mu, s…õ …îy…õ …îmamforanyi o, obi a w…îwoo no w…î asaase no do o.

ref: Stay away from yeast, no matter where you live. No one is allowed to eat anything made with yeast!
text: Mma hom nndzi biribiara a akaw; hom ndzi epitsi w…î hom tsenabew nyinara.

ref: Moses called the leaders of Israel together and said: Each family is to pick out a sheep and kill it for Passover.
text: Nna Moses fr…õ…õ Israel mpanyimfo nyinara, na …îsee h…în d…õ, Hom nk…î nkeyiyi nguambaa d…õ mbr…õ hom ebusua tsetsee, na hom nku apaho no.

ref: Make a brush from a few small branches of a hyssop plant and dip the brush in the bowl that has the blood of the animal in it. Then brush some of the blood above the door and on the posts at each side of the door of your house. After this, everyone is to stay inside until morning.
text: Na hom mfa odwonhataw siaw, na hom mfa mb…î b…îgya a …îw…î b…õ…õsen no mu no mu, na hom mfa b…îgya a …îw…î b…õ…õsen no mu no bi mpetse mpondua etsifi nye apondua ebien no ho; na mma hom mu obiara mmfir edzi mmfi ne fie abowano ma onkodu anapa.

ref: During that night the Lord will go through the country of Egypt and kill the first-born son in every Egyptian family. He will see where you have put the blood, and he will not come into your house. His angel that brings death will pass over and not kill your first-born sons.
text: Na EWURADZE botwa mu ab…îb…î Egyptfo; na s…õ ohu b…îgya no w…î mpondua etsifi, nye apondua ebien no ho a, EWURADZE b…õpa …îpon no ho, na …înnk…õma …îs…õ…õfo no kwan ma …îaaba hom efi mu ab…îbob…î hom.

ref: After you have entered the country promised to you by the Lord, you and your children must continue to celebrate Passover each year.
text: Na …îb…õba d…õ, s…õ hom du asaase a EWURADZE dze b…õma hom no do, d…õ mbr…õ …îab…î anohoba no a, …îno hom ndzi …îsom yi do.

ref: Your children will ask you, ‚ÄúWhat are we celebrating?‚Äù
text: Na …îb…õba d…õ, s…õ hom mba bisa hom d…õ, Hom som yi ase nye d…õn?

ref: And you will answer, ‚ÄúThe Passover animal is killed to honor the Lord. We do these things because on that night long ago the Lord passed over the homes of our people in Egypt. He killed the first-born sons of the Egyptians, but he spared our children from death.‚Äù After Moses finished speaking, the people of Israel knelt down and worshiped the Lord.
text: ∆Üno hom nse d…õ, ∆Üy…õ EWURADZE n‚Äôapaho af…îrb…îdze, nyia …îpaa Israelmba h…în efi ho w…î Egypt, w…î aber a …îb…î…î Egyptfo, na oyii h…õn efi fii mu no. Na …îman no b…î…î h…în tsir adze na w…îs…îree.

ref: Then they left and did what Moses and Aaron had told them to do. Death for the First-Born Sons
text: Na Israelmba k…îree k…õy…õ…õ d…õ mbr…õ EWURADZE hy…õ…õ Moses nye Aaron no. Mbakamba H…în Wu

ref: At midnight the Lord killed the first-born son of every Egyptian family, from the son of the king to the son of every prisoner in jail. He also killed the first-born male of every animal that belonged to the Egyptians.
text: Na …îbaa d…õ esuom no, EWURADZE b…îb…î…î Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara, ofi Farao n‚Äôabakan a …îtsena n‚Äôahengua do no do dze kodu adaduadan mu nd…îmmum n‚Äôabakan do; nye ofiembowa mbakan nyinara.

ref: That night the king, his officials, and everyone else in Egypt got up and started crying bitterly. In every Egyptian home, someone was dead. The People of Israel Escape from Egypt
text: Na Farao so…õ…õr anadwe no ara, …îno, nye no nkowaa nyinara, nye Egyptfo nyinara: na su k…õse baa Egypt; osiand…õ fie biara nnyi h…î a obi ennwu w…î mu.

ref: During the night the king sent for Moses and Aaron and told them, ‚ÄúGet your people out of my country and leave us alone! Go and worship the Lord, as you have asked.
text: Na …îfr…õ…õ Moses nye Aaron anadwe, na …îsee d…õ, Hom nso…õr, hom mfi me man mu mfir, hom nye Israelmba no nyina; na hom nk…î nk…îsom EWURADZE, d…õ mbr…õ hom aka no.

ref: Take your sheep, goats, and cattle, and get out. But ask your God to be kind to me.‚Äù
text: Hom mfa hom nguan nye hom anantwi so, d…õ mbr…õ hom aka no, na hom nk…î; na hom nhyira mo so.

ref: The Egyptians did everything they could to get the Israelites to leave their country as quickly as possible. They said, ‚ÄúPlease hurry and leave. If you don't, we will all be dead.‚Äù
text: Na Egyptfo no piapiaa …îman no d…õ w…îrepam h…în nts…õm efi asaase no do; na w…îsee d…õ, H…õn nyinara ay…õ ewufo ara nye no.

ref: So the Israelites quickly made some bread dough and put it in pans. But they did not mix any yeast in the dough to make it rise. They wrapped cloth around the pans and carried them on their shoulders.
text: Na …îman no fefaa h…în mb…îr ansaana …îrekaw, h…în mb…îrakorow no w…îakyekyer no w…î h…în atam mu sisi h…în k…în ho.

ref: The Israelites had already done what Moses had told them to do. They had gone to their Egyptian neighbors and asked for gold and silver and for clothes.
text: Na Israelmba no y…õ…õ d…õ mbr…õ Moses kae no; na wobisabisaa dwet…õ ndwindze, nye sika ndwindze, nye atam w…î Egyptfo h…î:

ref: The Lord had made the Egyptians friendly toward the people of Israel, and they gave them whatever they asked for. In this way they carried away the wealth of the Egyptians when they left Egypt.
text: na EWURADZE maa …îman no enyimpa w…î Egyptfo no enyim, ma w…îmaa h…în nsa kaa dza wobisae no. Na w…îfoom Egyptfo egyapadze k…îree. Israelmba No Fi Egypt

ref: The Israelites walked from the city of Rameses to the city of Succoth. There were about
text: Na Israelmba no sii mu fii Rameses k…îr Suk…ît, mbanyin ara a w…înam famu b…õy…õ mpem ahaesia, a mbofraba nnka ho.

ref: Many other people went with them as well, and there were also a lot of sheep, goats, and cattle.
text: Na aforafora d…îm so nye h…în k…îree; na nguan, na anantwi, mekyer…õ ofiembowa piigyan.

ref: They left Egypt in such a hurry that they did not have time to prepare any food except the bread dough made without yeast. So they baked it and made thin bread.
text: Na w…îdze mb…îr a w…îdze fi Egypt bae no totoo epitsi tatar, nna …înnkawee; osiand…õ w…îpaam h…în fii Egypt ntsi, na woenntum enntsinntsim, nkyii so woennsiesie edzidzidze biara amma h…înho.

ref: On that night the Lord kept watch for them, and on this same night each year Israel will always keep watch in honor of the Lord. Instructions for Passover
text: ∆Üy…õ anadwe a …îw…î d…õ w…îhy…õ no nsew ma EWURADZE d…õ oyii h…în fii Egypt asaase do: iyi nye EWURADZE n‚Äôanadwe no a w…îhy…õ no nsew w…î Israelmba nyinara mu w…î h…în ebusuasantsen nyina mu no. Apaho No Ho Nhyehy…õ…õ

ref: The Lord gave Moses and Aaron the following instructions for celebrating Passover: Only Israelites may eat the Passover meal.
text: Na EWURADZE see Moses nye Aaron d…õ, Iyi nye apaho no ho mbra no: …înanaben biara nndzi bi:

ref: Your slaves may eat the meal if they have been circumcised,
text: na nyimpa biara n‚Äôakowaa a …îdze sika at…î no no dze, s…õ etwa no twatsia a, …îno ondzi bi.

ref: but no foreigners who work for you are allowed to have any.
text: Mma …îmamforanyi nye akowaa …îpaanyi nndzi bi.

ref: The entire meal must be eaten inside, and no one may leave the house during the celebration. No bones of the Passover lamb may be broken.
text: Ofi kor mu na wondzi; mma mmfa nam no bi mmfi fie no mu mmpue; nkyii so mma hom mmbu ne biew bi.

ref: And all Israelites must take part in the meal.
text: Israel mboanofo nyinara ndzi do.

ref: If anyone who isn't an Israelite wants to celebrate Passover with you, every man and boy in that family must first be circumcised. Then they may join in the meal, just like native Israelites. No uncircumcised man or boy may eat the Passover meal!
text: Na s…õ …îh…îho bi b…õnye wo tsena, na …îp…õ d…õ odzi apaho no ma EWURADZE a, ma wontwa ne mbarimba nyinara twatsia, na nkyii …întwe mb…õn na ombedzi; na …îny…õ d…õ nyia w…îawo no asaase no do: monomonomnyi biara dze mma onndzi bi.

ref: This law applies both to native Israelites and to those foreigners who live among you.
text: Mbra kor na …înda h…î mma nyia …îy…õ okuromnyi, nye …îh…îho a …îtsena hom mu.

ref: The Israelites obeyed everything the Lord had commanded Moses and Aaron to tell them.
text: Na Israelmba nyinara y…õ…õ d…õm; d…õ mbr…õ EWURADZE hy…õ…õ Moses nye Aaron no, d…õmara so na w…îy…õe.

ref: And on that same day the Lord brought Israel's families and tribes out of Egypt.
text: Na …îbaa d…õ da kor no ara, EWURADZE yii Israelmba no fii Egypt asaase do d…õ mbr…õ h…în d…îm tsetsee.

ref: The Lord said to Moses,
text: Na EWURADZE kaa kyer…õ…õ Moses d…õ,

ref: ‚ÄúDedicate to me the first-born son of every family and the first-born males of your flocks and herds. These belong to me.‚Äù The Festival of Thin Bread
text: Tu mbakan nyinara si nky…õn ma me, adze biara a obue yafun mu, w…î Israelmba mu, w…î onyimpa nye abowa mu nyinara; y…õ medze. Epitsi Afahy…õ

ref: The Lord promised your ancestors that he would bring you into the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Hivites, and Jebusites. It is a land rich with milk and honey. Each year during the month of Abib, celebrate these events in the following way:
text: Na …îb…õba d…õ s…õ EWURADZE nya dze wo du Keenanfo, Hitifo, Amorifo, Hivifo, nye Jebusfo asaase a …îkaa ho ntam kyer…õ…õ w‚Äôegyanom d…õ …îdze b…õma wo no, asaase a numfonsu nye ewo pem w…î do no do a, …îno som …îsom yi w…î bosoom yi mu.

ref: For seven days you are to eat bread made without yeast, and on the seventh day you are to celebrate a festival in honor of the Lord.
text: Nda esuon na dzi epitsi boodoo, na da a otsia esuon no b…õy…õ afahy…õ ama EWURADZE.

ref: During those seven days, you must not eat anything made with yeast or even have yeast anywhere near your homes.
text: Wondzi epitsi boodoo nda esuon no mu nyina; na mma wonnhu mb…îrkaw boodoo w…î wo nky…õn, nkyii so mma wonnhu mb…îrkaw w…î wo nky…õn, w…î wo ntoaado nyinara mu.

ref: Then on the seventh day you must explain to your children that you do this because the Lord brought you out of Egypt.
text: Na da no kyer…õ mu kyer…õ wo ba d…õ, Osian dza EWURADZE y…õe maa me w…î ber a mirifi Egypt aba no ntsi a.

ref: This celebration will be like wearing a sign on your hand or on your forehead, because then you will pass on to others the teaching of the Lord, whose mighty power brought you out of Egypt.
text: Na …îb…õy…õ wo wo nsa do as…õnkyer…õdze, na w‚Äôenyiwa ntamu nkaadze, ama EWURADZE mbra no aako w‚Äôanomu: na nsa a …îy…õ dzen na EWURADZE dze eyi wo efi Egypt.

ref: Celebrate this festival each year at the same time.
text: D…õm ntsi dzi mbra yi do w…î ne ber mu afrenhyia daa.

ref: The Lord will give you the land of the Canaanites, just as he promised you and your ancestors.
text: Na …îb…õba d…õ s…õ EWURADZE dze wo du Keenanfo asaase do, d…õ mbr…õ …îkaa ntam kyer…õ…õ …îwo nye w‚Äôegyanom no, na …îdze ma wo a,

ref: From then on, you must give him every first-born son from your families and every first-born male from your animals, because these belong to him.
text: …îno yi dza obue yafun mu nyinara si nky…õn ma EWURADZE, na abakan biara a ew…î a ofi abowa mu: enyin no ny…õ EWURADZE dze.

ref: But if something happens to the animal while the owner is present, you do not have to replace it. If you had leased the animal, the money you paid the owner will cover any harm done to it. Laws for Everyday Life The Lord said:
text: S…õ adze no wura no no ho w…î mu a, mma …înnhy…õ ananmu: s…õ …îy…õ apaab…î adze a, na ogyina n‚Äôapaab…îdze do. Abrab…î Na Nyamesom Ho Mbra

ref: Since Aaron's sons are priests, they should also look dignified. So make robes, sashes, and special caps for them.

text: Na y…õ ndwera ntwontwo ma h…în, ma …înkata h…în adagyaw do, mfi asen nkodu nser…õekyir:

text: nye epitsi boodoo, nye epitsi tatar a w…îdze ngo afor…îw, na epitsi tatar ntr…õtraba a w…îasera ho ngo: ewifua pa isiam na fa y…õ.

text: Na fa af…îrmbukyia no do b…îgya no bi, nye …îserango no bi, na fa petse Aaron do, nye ne ntar do, nye ne mbabanyin no do, nye ne mbabanyin no h…în ntar do ka no ho, ma …întsew …îno nye ne ntar, nye ne mbabanyin no, nye ne mbabanyin no h…în ntar ho nka no ho.

text: Na fa Aaron n‚Äôetuhoaky…õ oguannyin no ne nyan, na hihim y…õ EWURADZE enyim ohim af…îr: na …îny…õ wo ky…õp…õn.

ref: Repeat this ordination ceremony for Aaron and his sons seven days in a row, just as I have instructed you.
text: Na d…õm na fa y…õ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no d…õ mbr…õ mahy…õ wo no nyinara: nda esuon na tu h…în si nky…õn.

ref: Do this for seven days, and the altar will become so holy that anyone who touches it will become holy. Daily Sacrifices (Leviticus
text: Nda esuon na pata w…î af…îrmbukyia no ne tsir, na tsew ho: na af…îrmbukyia no ny…õ kr…înkr…în mu kr…înkr…în; biribiara a …îka af…îrmbukyia no …îny…õ kr…înkr…în.

text: Eguambaa kor na fa b…î af…îr anapa; na eguambaa kor so fa b…î af…îr ewimbir:

text: Na eguambaa kor no so fa b…î af…îr ewimbir, na y…õ no d…õ mbr…õ anapa edzidzi af…îr no, na mbr…õ no nom af…îr no tse, ma …îny…õ adze huanhuam, af…îr a w…îdze gya b…î ma EWURADZE.

ref: Because of who I am, the tent will become sacred, and Aaron and his sons will become worthy to serve as my priests.
text: Na meb…õtsew ehyiadzibuw no, nye af…îrmbukyia no ho: Aaron so nye ne mbabanyin no meb…õtsew h…în ho, ma woeedzi m‚Äôas…îfoe.

ref: I will live among you as your God,
text: Na meb…õtsena Israelmba no mu, na may…õ h…în Nyankop…în.

ref: and you will know that I am the Lord your God, the one who rescued you from Egypt, so that I could live among you.
text: Na wobohu d…õ emi nye EWURADZE h…în Nyankop…în a miyii h…în fii Egypt asaase do bae, d…õ meb…õtsena h…în mu no: emi nye EWURADZE h…în Nyankop…în.

ref: It is truly holy because it is dedicated to me, so don't ever make any for yourselves.
text: Na eduhuam hyewee a ereb…õy…õ no, mma hom nnsusu no mforaforae do nny…õ bi mma homankasa homho: …îny…õ wo kr…înkr…în mma EWURADZE.

ref: the altar for sacrifices, and the large bowl with its stand.
text: nye …îhyew af…îr af…îrmbukyia no nye no ho ndz…õmba no nyinara, nye eguars…õn no nye ne ntaease.

text: Na y…õ af…îrmbukyia a w…îhyew do eduhuam: akeesia dua na fa y…õ.

text: Na fa sika mapa dura ho, n‚Äôetsifi, nye ne nky…õn nky…õnmu hyia, nye ne mmb…õn no; na y…õ ho sika hankra fa hyia.

ref: Then below the edging on opposite sides attach two gold rings through which you can put the poles for carrying the altar.
text: Na y…õ ho sika nkaba ebien w…î ne hankra no ase, w…î ne mparba ebien do, ne nky…õnmu ebien no na y…õ no w…î do; na …îny…õ mpoma anan mu na w…îdze aasoa.

ref: These poles are also to be made of acacia wood covered with gold.
text: Na fa akeesia dua sinsian mpoma no, na duradura ho sika.

ref: Put the altar in front of the inside curtain of the sacred tent. The chest with the place of mercy is kept behind that curtain, and I will talk with you there.
text: Na fa si ntwamutam a …îw…î dase adaka no ho no enyim, w…î mb…îb…îrhu egua no a …îw…î dase no do no enyim, mbr…õ menye wo behyia no.

ref: Burn only the proper incense on the altar and never use it for grain sacrifices or animal sacrifices or drink offerings.

ref: After this, the priest will remove the fat from the bull and send it up in smoke on the altar. Finally, he will burn its remains outside the camp, just as he did with the other bull. By this sacrifice the sin of the whole nation will be forgiven. When a Tribal Leader Sins The Lord God said:

text: na s…õ w…îma ohu no b…în a …îafom w…î mu no a, …îmfa n‚Äôay…õy…õdze a …îy…õ ap…înkye mbra, onyin a onndzii dz…õm;

text: Na s…õ …îman mba no bi ammfa enyidaho na …îy…õ b…în w…î ndz…õmba nyinara a EWURADZE ahy…õ ho mbra d…õ mma w…înny…õ no mu biara a, odzi so.

ref: After this, all of the fat must be removed, just as when an animal is sacrificed to ask my blessing. Then the priest will send it up in smoke to me, together with a food offering, and your sin will be forgiven.

ref: We have obeyed the Lord in everything that has been done today, so that your sins may be forgiven.

text: Na …îbaa d…õ da a otsia aw…îtwe no, Moses fr…õ…õ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no, na Israel mpanyimfo no;

text: Nna w…îtwee b…õnee, na w…îsoasoaa h…în w…î h…în sorntar mu k…îr nseraban no ekyir; d…õ mbr…õ Moses kae no.

ref: Then Moses told Aaron and his other two sons, Eleazar and Ithamar: Don't show your sorrow by messing up your hair and tearing your priestly clothes, or the Lord will get angry. He will kill the three of you and punish everyone else. It's all right for your relatives, the people of Israel, to mourn for those he destroyed by fire.
text: Na Moses kaa kyer…õ…õ Aaron, nye ne mbabanyin Eleazar nye Itamar, d…õ, Mma hom mmb…î hom tsinhwi mp…õs…õw, nkyii so mma hom nnsunsuan hom ntar mu; amma hom eennwuwu, amma EWURADZE n‚Äôebufuw ammba mboanofo no nyinara do; mbom hom mma hom nuanom, Israel fi nyinara, ngyam …îhyew a EWURADZE dze gya ahyew h…în yi.

ref: But you are the Lord's chosen priests, and you must not leave the sacred tent, or you will die. Aaron and his two sons obeyed Moses.
text: Na mma hom mmfi ehyiadzibuw no n‚Äôabowano, amma hom eennwuwu: na EWURADZE …îserango no w…î hom do. Na w…îy…õ…õ d…õ mbr…õ Moses kae no. As…îfo No H…în Mbra

ref: The Lord said to Aaron:
text: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyer…õ…õ Aaron d…õ,

ref: When you or your sons enter the sacred tent, you must never drink beer or wine. If you do, you will die right there! This law will never change.
text: Mma nnom wenyin anaa nsa dzendzen, …îwo nye wo mbabanyin nyinara, aber a hom rok…î ehyiadzibuw no mu no, amma hom eennwuwu: …îny…õ daa ahy…õdze w…î hom ebusuasantsen nyinara mu:

ref: You must learn the difference between what is holy and what isn't holy and between the clean and the unclean.
text: na hom dze he aato adzekr…înkr…în nye adze a …înny…õ kr…înkr…în ntamu, nye adze a no ho nntsew nye dza no ho tsew ntamu;

ref: You must also teach the people of Israel everything that I commanded Moses to say to them.
text: na hom aakyer…õkyer…õ Israelmba ahy…õdze a EWURADZE nam Moses nsa do aka akyer…õ h…în no nyinara.

ref: Moses told Aaron and his two sons, Eleazar and Ithamar: The grain sacrifice that was offered to give thanks to the Lord is very holy. So make bread without yeast from the part that wasn't sent up in smoke and eat it beside the altar.
text: Na Moses kasaa kyer…õ…õ Aaron, na Eleazar nye Itamar, ne mbabanyin a w…îkae no d…õ, Hom mfa edziban af…îr a …îka a ofi EWURADZE ogya af…îr do no, na hom nkwetsi mb…îrkaw ndzi no w…î af…îrmbukyia ho: osiand…õ …îy…õ kr…înkr…îndze mu adzekr…înkr…în:

ref: The Lord has said that this belongs to you and your sons, and that it must be eaten in a holy place.
text: na hom ndzi no w…î bea kr…înkr…în do, osiand…õ …îy…õ w‚Äôahy…õdze, nye wo mbabanyin ahy…õdze, w…î EWURADZE ogya af…îr do: na d…õm na w…îahy…õ.

ref: But the choice ribs and the hind leg that were lifted up may be eaten by your entire family, as long as you do so in an acceptable place. These parts are yours from the sacrifices that the people offer to ask the Lord's blessing. This is what the Lord has commanded, and it will never change.
text: ∆Ümado aser…õ no nye …îtow nyan no w…îmfa nka ogya af…îr seradze no ho mbra, mb…îtow no …îtow af…îr w…î EWURADZE enyim; na …îny…õ wodze, nye wo mbabanyin a w…îka wo ho no dze, d…õ daa ahy…õdze; d…õ mbr…õ EWURADZE ahy…õ no.

ref: When Moses asked around and learned that the ram for the sin sacrifice had already been burned on the altar, he became angry with Eleazar and Ithamar and said,
text: Na Moses hwehw…õ…õ b…în ho af…îr ap…înkye no enyiberdo, na hw…õ, w…îahyew no: na no bo fuuw Eleazar na …înye Itamar, Aaron ne mbabanyin a w…îkae no, d…õ,

ref: ‚ÄúWhy didn't you eat the meat from this sacrifice in an acceptable place? It is very holy, and the Lord has given you this sacrifice to remove Israel's sin and guilt.
text: Eb…õn ntsi na hom enndzi b…în ho af…îr no w…î beebi mbr…õ …îy…õ bea kr…înkr…în no, osiand…õ …îy…õ kr…înkr…îndze mu adzekr…înkr…în, na …îdze ama hom d…õ, hom nsoa mboanofo no h…în mbusu, ny…õ mpata mma h…în w…î EWURADZE enyim?

ref: Whenever an animal's blood isn't brought into the sacred tent, I commanded you to eat its meat in an acceptable place, but you burned it instead.‚Äù
text: Hw…õ, w…îammfa no b…îgyaa no ammba kr…înkr…înbea no no mu: ampaara …îsee d…õ nky…õ hom ndzi no w…î kr…înkr…în bea no do h…î, d…õ mbr…õ mehy…õe no.

ref: Their father Aaron replied, ‚ÄúToday two of my sons offered the sacrifice for sin and the sacrifice to please the Lord, and look what has happened to me! Would the Lord have approved if I had eaten the sacrifice for sin?‚Äù
text: Na Aaron kasaa kyer…õ…õ Moses d…õ, Hw…õ, nd…õ da yi na w…îdze h…în b…în ho af…îr nye h…în …îhyew af…îr aba EWURADZE enyim; na ndz…õmba a …îtsetse d…õm yi aba mo do yi: na s…õ midzi b…în ho af…îr nd…õ a, ana nky…õ …îb…õy…õ enyis…î w…î EWURADZE enyim?

ref: Moses was satisfied with Aaron's reply.
text: Na Moses tsee no, …îy…õ…õ no abodwe.

ref: any animal that has divided hoofs and chews the cud.
text: Anansambowa mu biara a w…îapaa n‚Äôanansa mu, na …îy…õ anansapaae koraa, na …îkosa adzepuwii no, …îno na hom ndzi.

ref: The Lord told Moses and Aaron
text: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyer…õ…õ Moses nye Aaron, see h…în d…õ,

ref: to say to the community of Israel: You may eat
text: hom nkasa nkyer…õ Israelmba d…õ, Iyinom nye mbowa a hom ndzi w…î asaase do mbowa nyinara mu.

ref: The only winged insects you may eat are locusts, grasshoppers, and crickets. All other winged insects that crawl are too disgusting for you to eat.
text: Na ntumbowa menyamenya nyinara a w…îw…î anan anan anan no, y…õ ekyiwadze ma hom.

ref: Moles, rats, mice, and all kinds of lizards are unclean.
text: nye ban yer abamba, nye …îmampam, nye …îsamanapa, nye anhweaketsew, nye abosomanketsew.

ref: Anyone who touches their dead bodies or anything touched by their dead bodies becomes unclean until evening.
text: Iyinom nye h…în a h…în ho nntsew mma hom w…î dza w…îwea nyinara mu: s…õ wowu a, obiara a …îka h…în no, no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir.

ref: If something made of wood, cloth, or leather touches one of their dead bodies, it must be washed, but it is still unclean until evening.
text: Na adze biara a h…în mu dza oewu at…î do no, no ho nntsew; dua adze o, atar o, nwoma o, b…ît…î o, adze biara a w…îdze y…õ edwuma biara, w…îmfa nhy…õ nsu mu, na no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir; ansaana no ho atsew.

ref: If any of these animals is found dead in a clay pot, the pot must be broken to pieces, and everything in it becomes unclean.
text: Na d…õtses…õn biara a h…în mu bi b…ît…î mu no, adze biara a …îw…î mu no no ho nntsew, na s…õn no hom mb…î no.

ref: If you pour water from this pot on any food, that food becomes unclean, and anything drinkable in the pot becomes unclean.
text: No mu edziban a wodzi biara a nsu bogu mu no, no ho nntsew: na anondze nyinara a w…înom w…î d…õm adze biara mu no, no ho nntsew:

ref: If the dead body of one of these animals touches anything else, including ovens and stoves, that thing becomes unclean and must be destroyed.
text: Na adze biara a h…în fun no bi b…ît…î do no no ho nntsew; fononoo o, mbukyia o, wombubu no esinesin: h…în ho nntsew, na h…în ho nntsew mma hom.

ref: A spring or a cistern where one of these dead animals is found is still clean, but anyone who touches the animal becomes unclean.
text: Naaso nsubura anaa amona a nsu egyina mu no dze, no ho tsew: mbom dza …îka h…în fun no, no ho nntsew.

ref: If the dead body of one of these animals is found lying on seeds that have been set aside for planting, the seeds remain clean.
text: Na s…õ h…în fun bi t…î ekuadze a woedua biara do a, no ho tsew.

ref: But seeds that are soaking in water become unclean, if the dead animal is found in the water.
text: Mbom s…õ w…îdze nsu gu ekuadze no do a, na h…în fun bi t…î do a, ekuadze no ho nntsew mma hom.

ref: If an animal that may be eaten happens to die, and you touch it, you become unclean until evening.
text: Na s…õ abowa biara a hom dzi wu a, nyia …îka no fun no, no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir.

ref: If you eat any of its meat or carry its body away, you must wash your clothes, but you are still unclean until evening.
text: Na nyia odzi no fun bi no, …înhor ne ntar, na no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir: onyia so a …îsoa no fun no, nhor ne ntar, na no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir.

ref: If you eat any of them, you will become just as disgusting and unclean as they are.
text: Mma hom nny…õ homho ekyiwadze w…î aweaweabowa biara a …îwea ho, nkyii so mma hom nny…õ homho fi a …îmma hom ho nntsew w…î h…în ho.

ref: I am the Lord your God, and you must dedicate yourselves to me and be holy, just as I am holy. Don't become disgusting by eating any of these unclean creatures.
text: Na emi nye EWURADZE hom Nyankop…în: d…õm ntsi hom ntsew homho, na hom ny…õ kr…înkr…în; osiand…õ mey…õ kr…înkr…în. Nkyii so mma hom nngu homho fi w…î aweaweabowa biara a otwuw noho adze w…î asaase do no ho.

ref: I brought you out of Egypt so that I could be your God. Now you must become holy, because I am holy!
text: Na emi nye EWURADZE a miyii hom fii Egypt asaase do bae, d…õ mey…õ hom Nyankop…în no; d…õm ntsi hom ny…õ kr…înkr…în, na mey…õ kr…înkr…în.

ref: I have given these laws so that you will know what animals, birds, and fish are clean and may be eaten, and which ones are unclean and may not be eaten.
text: ma w…îdze apa adze a no ho nntsew nye dza no ho tsew mu, na adze a nkwa w…î mu a wodzi nye dza nkwa w…î mu a wonndzi mu.

ref: The Lord told Moses
text: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyer…õ…õ Moses d…õ,

ref: to say to the community of Israel: If a woman gives birth to a son, she is unclean for seven days, just as she is during her monthly period.
text: Kasa kyer…õ Israelmba d…õ, S…õ …îbaa bi nyinsen na …îwo banyimba a, …îno no ho nntsew araa nda esuon; d…õ mbr…õ no ho nntsew w…î n‚Äôasabu nda no mu no.

ref: Her son must be circumcised on the eighth day,
text: Na da a otsia aw…îtwe no wontwa ne banyinwer no twatsia.

ref: but her loss of blood keeps her from being completely clean for another
text: Na …înko ntandan mu adaduasa nda ebiasa w…î n‚Äôahoguar b…îgya mu; mma …înnka adzekr…înkr…în biara, nkyii so …îmmba kr…înkr…în bea, kyer…õ n‚Äôahoguar nda ewie du ana.

ref: Any woman who gives birth to a daughter is unclean for two weeks, just as she is during her period. And she won't be completely clean for another
text: Mbom s…õ …îwo basiaba a, …îno no ho nntsew araa adap…õn ebien, d…õ n‚Äôasabu mu: na …înko ntandan mu adaduosia nda esia w…î n‚Äôahoguar b…îgya mu.

ref: When the mother has completed her time of cleansing, she must come to the front of the sacred tent and bring to the priest a year-old lamb as a sacrifice to please me and a dove or a pigeon as a sacrifice for sin.
text: Na s…õ n‚Äôahoguar nda no wie du, ma …îbanyin, anaa …îbabaa a, …îmfa eguambaa a oedzi afrenhyia a w…îdze b…îb…î …îhyew af…îr, nye abor…înoma ba, anaa atamram a w…îdze b…îb…î b…în ho af…îr, mbra ehyiadzibuw no n‚Äôabowano mb…õma …îs…îfo no:

ref: After the priest offers the sacrifices to me, the mother will become completely clean from her loss of blood, whether her child is a boy or a girl.
text: na …îmfa mb…î af…îr w…î EWURADZE enyim, na …împata mma no; na no ho ntsew w…î no b…îgyaagu no ho. Iyi nye mbra a …îw…î h…î ma nyia …îwo …îbanyin anaa …îbaa.

ref: If she cannot afford a lamb, she can offer two doves or two pigeons, one as a sacrifice to please me and the other as a sacrifice for sin.
text: Na s…õ ne nsa annso eguambaa do a, …îno …îmfa ntamram ebien; kor ny…õ …îhyew af…îr, na kor so ny…õ b…în ho af…îr: na …îs…îfo no mpata mma no, na no ho ntsew.
